mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust and expanding economy. This strength translates into higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2016 PAYEMS showed a strong +255k print, continuing a positive trend.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate is a classic leading indicator of a recession. When the rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, it signals a cyclical downturn, causing a flight to safety and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns, invalidating the signal's directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation (rising CPIAUCSL) and decelerating industrial production (falling INDPRO) signals a stagflationary environment. This dynamic compresses corporate profit margins and increases discount rates, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY % change > 2.5% AND INDPRO YoY % change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:1970s stagflationary period was poor for equities.
failure_condition: A regression model of 60-day returns on the two proxies shows an insignificant or positive coefficient for their interaction term (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When monetary policy is already restrictive (FEDFUNDS > 4%), any further increase in the policy rate signals the central bank's intent to slow the economy significantly. This action sharply increases the probability of a recession, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The current FEDFUNDS rate of 0.39% is far below the hypothetical trigger.
regime_hint: high interest rate / monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: When the regime condition (FEDFUNDS > 4%) is met, triggered rate hikes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: Strong acceleration in retail sales reflects robust consumer demand, which translates directly to higher expected revenues for S&P 500 companies and drives positive short-term returns as earnings estimates are revised upwards.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest retail sales data showed strength, supporting the consumer narrative.
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (predicting positive 5-day returns when RSAFS 3-month change is positive) falls below 55% over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year contraction in industrial production indicates a broad-based slowdown in the goods-producing sector of the economy. This is a strong signal of weakening aggregate demand and often precedes recessions, causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO had been showing YoY weakness through early 2016.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of all historical instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a key interest-rate sensitive, long-leading indicator of economic activity. A sharp month-over-month decline signals weakening investment and consumer confidence, negatively impacting equity market sentiment over the next month.
failure_condition: The correlation between the triggered signal and 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant and negative (p > 0.10) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating real GDP growth rate signals a healthy, expanding economy. This environment fosters strong corporate earnings growth and positive investor sentiment, leading to broad equity market gains over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ % change > GDPC1 4-quarter avg QoQ % change
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2016 GDP was recently released and showed weak growth.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from the average return when the trigger is not active (t-test p > 0.05).
